body.title: cmd_next marks in_progress at dispatch; sprint verifies not claims
body.result: cmd_next already marks in_progress atomically (confirmed at lines 1276-1314); updated /sprint-ticket Step 2 to verify-or-mark pattern (sprint→setstatus, in_progress→proceed, else→surface); 3 new tests green — TheIgors 64098b07
body.description:
The queue is an encapsulated black box. Workers request work; the act of dispatch IS the assignment — no separate claim step needed. Currently cmd_next returns a ticket ID but leaves status=sprint; worker then calls cmd_claim separately. Fix: cmd_next atomically marks the ticket in_progress (status + claimed_at + worker) when it returns the ID. /sprint-ticket Step 2 becomes a verify step: if ticket is already in_progress (dispatched by cmd_next), proceed; if still sprint (direct CC sprint by Akien), mark in_progress now. cmd_claim is already LegacyDirectClaimError — do not restore it.

**Affected files:** ~/TheIgors/lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (cmd_next, ~10 lines), ~/.claude/skills/sprint-ticket/SKILL.md (Step 2 rewrite)
**Design rules:** encapsulation — workers have one interface: request_work(); dispatch IS assignment
**Scope boundary:** cmd_next + sprint Step 2 only; worker_daemon.sh is unchanged (it already calls cmd_next); no new queue commands added
**Test plan:** cmd_next returns ID and immediately shows status=in_progress via cc_queue.py show; direct CC sprint on a sprint-status ticket also marks in_progress cleanly; 4 tests total
**Validation:** After cmd_next runs, cc_queue.py show T-xxx returns status=in_progress with claimed_at set; log line '[dispatch] T-xxx → {worker}' appears in channel
**Cleanup:** test fixtures use tmp queue JSON, reset after each test
**Logging:** log.info('[dispatch] {ticket_id} → {worker}') in cmd_next when ticket returned
**Observable via:** log.info('[dispatch] T-xxx → claude') in cc_queue.py:cmd_next
